methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DZYNE Technologies, a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Matt McCue, has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation exercise draws on a comp universe of 20 same-sector peers (2 public, 8 private identified), including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries, though narrow peer clustering constrains multiples-based triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $96M implied valuation. This approach uses the company's $80M total raised as a base value proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, reflecting expected value creation between funding rounds. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 20 Defense & Security peers exist in the broader universe, filtering for same-stage and adjacent-stage comps yielded insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3) to establish a reliable multiples baseline without introducing material selection bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $82M to $110M, with a median point of $96M. This represents a single-method triangulation; the primary uncertainty stems from the narrow applicability window—peer multiples remain constrained by stage and vertical concentration. Key sensitivities include customer concentration, contract duration/renewability, and technical validation milestones within the defense procurement cycle. Additional comparable transactions or disclosed growth metrics would sharpen conviction in this range.
